commands.repair_reason: Not a repair candidate: the code direction is sound, but the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof that automation cannot supply for their Twitch setup.; Cleared: The diff only changes Twitch registry cleanup logic and colocated tests; it adds no dependency, workflow, credential, install, or code-execution surface. (sha=69120f062996e80c19a43bb1a3101aa9e3227b77)
commands.review_summary:
The branch wraps Twitch `removeClientManager` teardown in `try/finally` so registry eviction always runs, and expands registry tests for failed `disconnectAll` cases.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main deletes the registry entry only after `disconnectAll()` completes, and the factory returns the cached manager if that entry remains. I did not run a failing current-main test because this review was explicitly read-only.
commands.review_followup: Not a repair candidate: the code direction is sound, but the remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof that automation cannot supply for their Twitch setup.

target.title: fix(twitch): evict registry entry even when disconnectAll throws (#83886)
target.body:
Fixes #83886.

`removeClientManager` in `extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.ts:75` is the public teardown entry point ‚Äî `plugin.ts:208` calls it when an account is stopped or reconfigured. The body is:

```ts
const entry = registry.get(accountId);
if (!entry) return;
await entry.manager.disconnectAll();
registry.delete(accountId);
```

If `disconnectAll()` rejects (a `quit()` call on a broken socket, an underlying tmi.js exception, etc.), `registry.delete(accountId)` is never reached. The entry survives in the module-level `Map`. The next `getOrCreateClientManager(accountId, ‚Ä¶)` short-circuits on the cache branch (`if (existing) return existing.manager`) and hands back the broken / partially-disconnected manager ‚Äî silent stale-state hazard on top of the original disconnect failure.

## Changes

- `extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.ts`: wrap `disconnectAll` in `try { ‚Ä¶ } finally { registry.delete; logger.info }`. The `finally` block is unconditional, so the registry is cleaned up whether the disconnect succeeded or threw. The original rejection still propagates to the caller (finally doesn't suppress it).
- `extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts`: NEW colocated test file. Mocks `TwitchClientManager` at the constructor boundary so `disconnectAll` can be driven to resolve or reject without standing up a real `@twurple/chat` client. Four cases ‚Äî happy path eviction, no-op when no entry exists, **regression: throwing `disconnectAll` still evicts**, and end-to-end: after a failed remove, `getOrCreateClientManager` constructs a fresh manager (not the stale one). Uses `vi.resetModules()` to start each test with an empty module-level `registry`, so the test file is self-contained and doesn't depend on a separate test-only export.

Diff stat: 2 files, +105 / -6. Behavior change is strictly safer: any case that previously resolved still does identically; the previously-broken throwing-disconnect path now leaves the registry in a clean state.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sanitized issue evidence ‚Äî `removeClientManager` is the sole caller-facing teardown for `registry`, and `registry` is the cache layer that `getOrCreateClientManager` short-circuits through. The eviction must be atomic with the teardown intent, not gated on a successful disconnect.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83886.mjs` does both halves of the proof. (a) Parses the patched `client-manager-registry.ts` and verifies (i) `removeClientManager` wraps `disconnectAll` in `try { ‚Ä¶ } finally { ‚Ä¶ }`, (ii) `registry.delete(accountId)` is inside the finally block, (iii) no remaining unguarded delete-after-disconnectAll path. (b) Replays the registry semantics in pure JS for four scenarios ‚Äî buggy shape (throwing `disconnectAll` ‚Üí registry retains `'acc-1'`, confirming the #83886 symptom), patched shape (throwing `disconnectAll` ‚Üí registry evicts `'acc-1'` AND error still propagates to caller), patched happy path (clean disconnect still evicts), and patched no-op (missing key returns without throw).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83886.mjs` AND `pnpm vitest run --no-coverage extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts`

- **Evidence after fix (real vitest output against patched `removeClientManager`):**

```
RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-scout

 ‚úì |extension-messaging| extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts > removeClientManager > removes the registry entry on the happy path 35ms
 ‚úì |extension-messaging| extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts > removeClientManager > is a no-op when no entry exists for the account 1ms
 ‚úì |extension-messaging| extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts > removeClientManager > evicts the registry entry even when disconnectAll throws (#83886) 1ms
 ‚úì |extension-messaging| extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.test.ts > removeClientManager > after a failed remove, getOrCreateClientManager constructs a fresh manager (#83886) 1ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Duration  249ms (transform 128ms, setup 118ms, import 3ms, tests 39ms, environment 0ms)
```

The four-case suite exercises the actual patched module:
- **happy path** ‚Äî clean `disconnectAll`, registry evicts as before.
- **no-op** ‚Äî missing key returns without throw.
- **#83886 regression** ‚Äî `disconnectAll` rejects ‚Üí registry MUST still evict AND the error propagates.
- **fresh-manager after failed remove** ‚Äî `getOrCreateClientManager` after a failed remove constructs a NEW manager bound to a NEW logger, not the broken cached one.

- **Probe replay (unchanged from prior body):**

```
PASS: removeClientManager now wraps disconnectAll in try { ‚Ä¶ } finally { ‚Ä¶ }
PASS: registry.delete(accountId) is called inside the finally block (always runs)
PASS: no remaining unguarded delete-after-disconnectAll path
PASS: replay (buggy): disconnectAll throws ‚Üí registry retains 'acc-1' (confirms #83886)
PASS: replay (patched): disconnectAll throws ‚Üí registry evicts 'acc-1' AND error still propagates
PASS: replay (patched, happy path): clean disconnectAll ‚Üí entry evicted as expected
PASS: replay (patched, missing key): no-op, no throw

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** A `removeClientManager(accountId)` call where the underlying client's `disconnectAll` throws still removes the entry from the module-level registry. The next `getOrCreateClientManager(accountId, ‚Ä¶)` call gets a fresh manager bound to whatever logger the caller passed, instead of the stale failed-disconnect instance. Production cache-hit behavior within a healthy session (same accountId, no remove) is unchanged.

- **What was not tested:** A live `@twurple/chat` client with a real broken WebSocket ‚Äî the included regression test mocks `disconnectAll` at the boundary, which is the same shape the broader test suite uses for this module (`twitch-client.test.ts` mocks `@twurple/chat` at the constructor level).

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules ‚Äî all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** No helper introduced ‚Äî `try { ‚Ä¶ } finally { ‚Ä¶ }` is the canonical primitive for "always run cleanup regardless of failure." The `registry.delete` call is the same operation the buggy path was attempting; only the placement moves. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `removeClientManager` has 1 production caller (`extensions/twitch/src/plugin.ts:208`, account-stop / account-reconfigure path). The new behavior is strictly safer: any case that previously resolved still does so; the previously-broken throwing-disconnect path now cleans up correctly. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** `gh pr list --search '83886 in:title,body'` and `gh pr list --search 'removeClientManager twitch'` return no open PRs. The issue timeline cross-references issue #83887 (which is a sibling clawpatch finding that I shipped via #84244 ‚Äî not a competing PR). PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -5 -- extensions/twitch/src/client-manager-registry.ts` shows `e1061a8b46 test(live): tolerate provider drift in release checks` ‚Äî unrelated. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A ‚Äî `Map.delete` and try/finally on locally-bound values.
